definition.name: def-positive-unital-map
definition.definition: positive unital map (def-positive-unital-map, refs/idel-2013/idel-2013.md:347-348): a linear map T:B(H)->B(H) is positive if T(a)>=0 whenever a>=0 (hence self-adjointness-preserving, restricts to B(H)_sa) and unital if T(1)=1; a positive unital map is a contraction on the self-adjoint part, ||T||=1 on B(H)_sa. Here C and Phi=Cj are positive unital maps.
